name: Valencia de Alcántara and Alpotrel
name_original: Valencia de Alcántara desde San Vicente de Alcántara – circular por el Embalse de Alpotrel
description: Cycle 36.9 miles (59.4 km) on this moderate road cycling route from San Vicente de Alcántara, passing the scenic Embalse de Alpotrel.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around San Vicente De Alcántara traverse a diverse landscape in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, Spain. The region is characterized by the Sierra de San Pedro, offering varied natural features and undulating terrain. Cyclists will encounter extensive cork oak forests, known as "Alcornocales," and traditional dehesas, which are agroforestry systems of oak woodlands and pastures. The area provides a mix of relatively flat sections and more demanding climbs within the sierra, suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around San Vicente De Alcántara The most popular road cycling route is Valencia de Alcántara and Alpotrel, a 36.9 miles (59.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 43 minutes to complete, passing by the scenic Alpotrel Reservoir. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Castillo de La Beltraneja – Alto do Casqueijal loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara, a difficult 56.5 miles (91.0 km) path that includes historical castle views. Local road cyclists also love the Roadbike loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara, a 41.4 miles (66.7 km) trail leading through the region's characteristic dehesas and cork oak forests, often completed in about 3 hours 6 minutes. Road cycling around San Vicente De Alcántara is defined by the Sierra de San Pedro, extensive cork oak forests, and traditional dehesas. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The routes in San Vicente De Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 5.0 stars from 9 reviews. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore San Vicente De Alcántara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: The road cycling routes around San Vicente de Alcántara traverse a diverse landscape. You'll encounter undulating dehesas (traditional oak woodlands and pastures) and extensive cork oak forests, characteristic of the region. The Sierra de San Pedro also provides varied natural features, offering a mix of relatively flat sections and more demanding climbs.

Q: Are there challenging climbs for experienced road cyclists in the region?
A: Yes, the Sierra de San Pedro offers more demanding climbs suitable for experienced road cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128710" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de La Beltraneja – Alto do Casqueijal loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 56.5-mile (91.0 km) path with significant elevation gain, providing a good challenge.

Q: What natural features can I see while road cycling in San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: While cycling, you can experience the unique landscapes of the Sierra de San Pedro, including vast cork oak forests and traditional dehesas. The region is also home to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5459047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alpotrel Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;, a scenic spot that some routes pass by, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056304663/valencia-de-alcantara-and-alpotrel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valencia de Alcántara and Alpotrel&lt;/a&gt; route.

Q: Are there historical landmarks or cultural sites accessible by road bike?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. You can cycle past impressive castles like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1574176" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Luna&lt;/a&gt;, which is featured on routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20128656" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de La Beltraneja – Castle of Luna loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also known for its megalithic monuments, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5322796" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Dolmen del Mellizo-Anta de la Marquesa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: The most pleasant weather for road cycling in San Vicente de Alcántara is typically during spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, temperatures are milder, making for more comfortable rides through the region's diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: Yes, many of the routes in the area are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19432908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31431135" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castelo de Vide loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: The road cycling routes in San Vicente de Alcántara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 5.0 stars from 9 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, from scenic dehesas and cork oak forests to challenging climbs in the Sierra de San Pedro, and the opportunity to explore historical sites.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural monuments to see on road cycling routes?
A: While road cycling routes primarily focus on the main roads, some natural monuments are within the broader region. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6545309" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabroeira Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/8181441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cork Oak "El Abuelo"&lt;/a&gt; are notable natural features in the area, though they might require a short detour from some road cycling paths.

Q: How many road cycling routes are available in San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: There are 13 road cycling routes available around San Vicente de Alcántara on komoot, offering a range of experiences. These include 5 moderate routes and 8 difficult routes, catering to various skill levels.

Q: Can I find routes that pass by the famous cork oak forests?
A: Yes, San Vicente de Alcántara is known as the "Cork Capital," and many routes traverse its extensive cork oak forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19432908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roadbike loop from Estación de San Vicente de Alcántara&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, leads directly through these characteristic dehesas and cork oak landscapes.

Q: Are there any routes that offer views of the Sierra de San Pedro?
A: The Sierra de San Pedro is a central natural feature of the region, and many routes offer views of its varied landscape. The more challenging routes, in particular, often ascend to elevated spots within the sierra, providing panoramic vistas of the surrounding pastures, meadows, and Mediterranean forests.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of road cycling routes in San Vicente de Alcántara?
A: The road cycling routes in San Vicente de Alcántara are categorized into moderate and difficult levels. There are 5 moderate routes, suitable for intermediate riders, and 8 difficult routes for those seeking more challenging climbs and longer distances.
tips: The fortress castle is a Muslim fortification dating from the 13th century, rebuilt by the Order of Alcántara and remodeled over the years due to the numerous Portuguese offensives.
Small church in the town of San Pedro made of stone which also has the square around it and some of the surrounding houses. The center is a few kilometers from the border with Portugal.

The Alpotrel Reservoir in San Vicente de Alcántara, located in the province of Badajoz, Extremadura, is a hydraulic infrastructure used primarily for irrigation and water supply. It is also a popular place for recreational activities such as fishing and hiking.
San Vicente de Alcántara is a small town on the border with Portugal, located between the provinces of Cácares and Badajoz on the slopes of the Sierra de Santa María.

faqs.question: Where does the trail start and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The starting point for the "Valencia de Alcántara from San Vicente de Alcántara – circular by the Embalse de Alpotrel" route is conveniently located next to a parking lot in San Vicente de Alcántara. This makes it easy to begin your ride.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This racebike route is approximately 59.4 km long with an elevation gain of about 692 meters. It typically takes around 2 hours and 43 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route primarily features mostly paved surfaces, making it well-suited for racebikes. You can expect a smooth ride for the majority of the journey.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' and requires good fitness due to its distance and elevation gain. While not overly strenuous, it's best suited for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the way?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll pass by several interesting spots. These include the Fortress Castle of Valencia de Alcántara, the Church of San Pedro de Alcántara, the scenic Alpotrel Reservoir, and the San Vicente de Alcántara Town Hall.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The region of Extremadura, where this route is located, generally offers pleasant conditions for cycling during spring and autumn. Summers can be quite hot, and winters might be cooler, so consider the weather for your comfort.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As this is a road cycling route primarily on paved surfaces, it is generally not suitable for dogs to accompany you on the bike itself. If you plan to bring a dog, ensure they can be safely transported or consider alternative activities.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It is freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Is this a circular cycling route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the "Valencia de Alcántara from San Vicente de Alcántara – circular by the Embalse de Alpotrel" is a circular route, meaning you will end your ride back at your starting point in San Vicente de Alcántara.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route briefly intersects with sections of other paths, including Molino de La Negra, Camino Zafra, Travesía Hernán Cortés, and Parque de España, among others. However, the primary focus is the circular route around the Embalse de Alpotrel.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or water along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through towns like Valencia de Alcántara and San Vicente de Alcántara, it's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when cycling in rural areas. You may find establishments in the towns for refreshments.
